title: Mencho/CJNG Update — Mar 28, 2026
summary: Day 34 of the post-Mencho succession crisis: 'O3' (Juan Carlos Valencia González) continues consolidating CJNG leadership under a collective council structure per U.S. NCTC assessment, with El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remaining an active rival power center. President Sheinbaum announced deployment of 100,000 security personnel for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Guadalajara — the heart of CJNG territory — under particular scrutiny. The Trump administration granted Mexico a 90-day tariff postponement as bilateral security cooperation continues, with over 55 cartel figures transferred to U.S. custody. A new contested claims entry examines whether Mexico can adequately secure Guadalajara for the World Cup given the ongoing CJNG succession crisis.

title: Mencho/CJNG Update — Mar 27, 2026
summary: Day 33 of the post-Mencho succession crisis: Argentina became one of the first Latin American nations to formally designate CJNG a terrorist organization, citing the cartel's presence in 40+ countries and its expanding footprint in Argentina, Ecuador, and Chile. 'El 03' (Juan Carlos Valencia González) continues consolidating CJNG leadership under a collective council structure as the succession vacuum enters its fifth week. Security analysts at Brookings and the Wilson Center are urging institutionalization of the U.S.-Mexico DEA-SEDENA intelligence framework that enabled the Tapalpa operation, warning that FY2026 Congressional conditionality provisions on Mexico counternarcotics funding risk undermining the bilateral relationship. The days-since-El-Mencho-killed KPI was updated to 33.

title: Mencho/CJNG Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Day 32 since El Mencho's killing: U.S.-born Juan Carlos Valencia González ('El 03') has been confirmed by the U.S. National Counterterrorism Center and State Department as CJNG's de facto leader, with a $5 million reward for his capture, though analysts caution the cartel continues under a collective council structure. Rival criminal organizations including Carteles Unidos and the Santa Rosa de Lima Cartel are intensifying pressure on CJNG strongholds in Michoacán and Guanajuato, exploiting the succession vacuum. CJNG has simultaneously advanced into Sinaloa Cartel territory amid that organization's ongoing internal fracture, with Los Chapitos reportedly forging a tactical alliance with CJNG. The U.S. Congress is debating $175 million in FY2026 counterfentanyl funding, with conditions on Mexico cooperation that analysts warn could strain the intelligence-sharing relationship that enabled the February 22 Tapalpa operation.

title: CJNG Crisis Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: Day 28 since the killing of El Mencho: CJNG's succession vacuum deepens as El Menchito remains imprisoned in the United States with no other confirmed heir. Security analysts warn of prolonged violent realignments lasting months to potentially years as criminal factions contest territorial control. Rival Carteles Unidos is moving to exploit the power vacuum in Michoacán, with armed confrontations reported across multiple municipalities. Widespread violence erupted across 20+ Mexican states in the immediate aftermath of El Mencho's killing, including hijackings, vehicle burnings, and attacks on businesses.
